diagnosis: The failing tests cluster into three main mechanisms: (1) Strigolactone biosynthesis mutants (CCD7, CCD8, D27, MAX1, MAX3, MAX4) all show inverted predictions, indicating a broken SL synthesis cascade; (2) D53 and SMXL6 KO/OE tests show incorrect or unchanged responses, revealing a missing SL signaling core module; (3) Decapitation and sucrose-related tests indicate miswired feedback between sugar signaling and BRC1. The root cause is a disconnected SL receptor module (D14/MAX2) from downstream effectors (SMXLs, BRC1), and incorrect regulation of BRC1 by key hormones.

fixes.rationale: Strigolactones induce degradation of D53 (E121, 10.1038/nature12870); this closes the core SL signaling loop and explains why D53 KO increases branching — without SL-induced degradation, D53 persists and represses branching. Fixes T053, T101, T131, T154.

D14 mediates degradation of D53 (E122, 10.1038/nature12870); this links receptor perception to D53 turnover, fixing the D14 KO cluster (T011, T056, T110) where current model fails to propagate signal.

Strigolactone promotes BRC1 expression (E030, 10.1016/j.tplants.2018.12.001); this corrects the direction of SL action upstream of BRC1, aligning with T004 (Decapitation KO) and biosynthesis mutants where SL loss should reduce BRC1 and increase branching.

Sucrose inhibits BRC1 expression (E021, 10.1093/jxb/erv047); this fixes sucrose-mediated promotion of branching (T153, T173) by ensuring sugar signals suppress BRC1, consistent with metabolic activation of buds.

MAX2 promotes BRC1 expression (E145, 10.3390/plants3020223); this restores the canonical SL-MAX2-BRC1 axis, correcting MAX2 OE failures (T026, T175) and biosynthesis KO tests where MAX2 function is essential.
